criteria: Define a CUDA kernel function using the __global__ qualifier with correct syntax
Configure grid and block dimensions for kernel launch using dim3 or integer arguments with ceiling division for arbitrary data sizes
Compute global thread index correctly using blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x formula
Allocate and free GPU memory using cudaMalloc and cudaFree with proper error handling
Perform bidirectional host-device memory transfers using cudaMemcpy with correct direction enum (cudaMemcpyHostToDevice, cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost)
Implement a working vector addition kernel with bounds checking for arbitrary array sizes
Check for CUDA errors using cudaGetLastError after kernel launch and cudaDeviceSynchronize for completion
Compile and execute CUDA programs using nvcc compiler
Explain the SIMT execution model and how warps of 32 threads execute in lockstep
Describe the host-device memory model and why explicit memory transfers are required
Implement coalesced global memory access with adjacent threads accessing adjacent 4-byte words aligned to 128-byte boundaries
Declare and use shared memory using __shared__ qualifier with proper indexing
Avoid shared memory bank conflicts through padding or access pattern modification
Benchmark memory bandwidth using CUDA events and report GB/s utilization
Implement tiled matrix transpose using shared memory with coalesced read and write phases
Compare naive vs optimized transpose performance with documented speedup metrics (target 10x+ improvement)
Correctly use __syncthreads() barrier between shared memory write and read phases
Handle non-power-of-2 matrix dimensions with proper bounds checking
Calculate and report achieved bandwidth as percentage of theoretical peak
Diagnose memory access patterns by tracing thread-to-address mapping
Implement tree-based parallel reduction using shared memory with O(log n) step complexity
Optimize reduction to eliminate warp divergence in final iterations using unrolled warp-synchronous code
Optionally implement warp shuffle-based reduction using __shfl_down_sync for improved performance
Implement Blelloch (work-efficient) exclusive scan with upsweep and downsweep phases
Handle arbitrary (non-power-of-2) array sizes in both reduction and scan implementations
Implement hierarchical scan for arrays larger than a single block using block-sum propagation
Implement privatized histogram using per-block shared memory histograms to reduce atomic contention
Understand and avoid atomic contention problems when using atomicAdd on shared counters
Benchmark algorithms against CPU implementations and demonstrate GPU speedup scales with data size
Identify and resolve shared memory bank conflicts in reduction and scan access patterns
